commentary_text: The model assigns Argentina a 76% win probability while the sportsbook consensus stands at 60%—a 16-percentage-point gap favoring the model's confidence in Argentina. The market also prices Austria substantially higher at 16% versus the model's 4%, suggesting real-money flows may be reflecting information about recent form, injuries, or lineup changes unavailable to the model's last update. Draw probabilities remain relatively aligned. The divergence reflects a classic pattern: the model's Elo-based framework sees a clear hierarchy, but the market hedges that view, implying material developments may have shifted expectations since the model was last trained.
